sanskrit: एतान्यपि तु कर्माणि सङ्गं त्यक्त्वा फलानि च । कर्तव्यानीति मे पार्थ निश्िचतं मतमुत्तमम् ॥
hindiTranslation: हे पार्थ  पूर्वोक्त यज्ञ, दान और तप   इन कर्मोंको तथा दूसरे भी कर्मोंको आसक्ति और फलोंका त्याग करके करना चाहिये  यह मेरा निश्चित किया हुआ उत्तम मत है ॥
englishTranslation: Even these actions, and all other actions, should be done without attachment and without wanting their results. This is my settled and best teaching, O Arjuna.
essence: Right action is complete only when desire for reward is dropped.
speaker: Krishna
simpleMeaning: Even sacred actions and all other duties should be done without clinging to results. Krishna says this is the best and settled teaching.
simpleInsight: You can give your full effort without carrying the burden of control.
detailedMeaning: On the battlefield, Arjuna is frozen between duty and fear. Krishna continues his teaching on renunciation, making one thing clear: even offerings, charity, and discipline should be done without attachment to results. This verse sharpens the path of action.
modernRelevance: You send the message, make the apology, or hand in the project, then sit with the uncertainty. The value was in doing it rightly, not in controlling how it lands.
translationLiteral: एतानि (these) / अपि (also) / तु (indeed) / कर्माणि (actions) / सङ्गं (attachment) / त्यक्त्वा (having given up) / फलानि (results) / च (and) / कर्तव्यानीति (ought to be done) / मे (my) / पार्थ (O Partha) / निश्चितं (settled) / मतम् (opinion/teaching) / उत्तमम् (best)
insight: It shifts action from outcome-hunger to clean participation in what must be done.

quoteVariants.text: Do the work. Release the result.
The work is yours. The result is not.
